name: Irish Coffee
directions: In a 2-cup measure combine water and instant coffee crystals.
Micro-cook, uncovered, on 100% power about 4 minutes or just till steaming hot.
Stir in Irish whiskey and brown sugar.
Serve in mugs.
Top each mug of coffee mixture with some pressurized dessert topping.

similar.name: Irish Coffee from the Buena Vista San Francisco

name: Irish Coffee
directions: Rinse a stemmed glass with hot water.
Put the sugar in the glass, followed by whiskey.
Add coffee, leaving room for the cream.
Stir thoroughly and let settle, before pouring the cream on top over the back of a spoon so it just floats.

similar.name: Irish Coffee from the Buena Vista San Francisco

name: Dad's Irish Coffee
directions: Pour enough Creme de Cacao in the bottom of a coffee cup to just coat the bottom of the cup.
Add the Irish whiskey.
Fill the cup with coffee leaving room for the whipped cream.
Using a swirling motion, top the coffee with whipped cream.
Drizzle Creme de Cocoa on the whipped cream.
Enjoy.

similar.name: Irish Coffee from the Buena Vista San Francisco

name: Turkish Coffee
directions: In an open kettle about the size of a large mug, boil water and sugar, add up to 2 tsp for very sweet coffee.
Lower the flame and add the coffee.
Bring the mixture just to a boil the coffee but don't allow to spill over.
Pour some froth into each coffee cup.
Bring the mixture just to a boil again and pour evenly into turkish coffee cups.

name: Irish Coffee
directions: Warm two glasses.  In each put 1 teaspoon sugar and 2 shots of whiskey, then the coffee.  Stir gently, then flow 2 Tablespoons of the cream into each cup over the back of a spoon, so that it remains floating on the top.  Serve immediately, without stirring.
NOTE:  Not only does Irish whiskey taste different from Scotch, it is spelled differently: "whiskey" versus "whisky".
Moira Hodgson.Holiday Recipes.

similar.name: Irish Coffee from the Buena Vista San Francisco

name: Irish Kiss Coffee
directions: Pour liqueurs and whiskey into Irish coffee glass.
Add coffee and stir.
Top with whipped cream and garnish with a chocolate covered mint.

similar.name: Irish Coffee from the Buena Vista San Francisco

name: Spanish Coffee
directions: Pour Tia Maria and rum into a coffee mug. Fill with hot coffee.
Top with whipped cream and a cherry.
Sit back and relax!

name: Irish Coffee from the Buena Vista San Francisco
directions: Fill glass with very hot water to pre-heat, then empty.
Pour hot coffee into hot glass until it is about three-quarters full.
Drop in two cocktail sugar cubes.
Stir until the sugar is thoroughly dissolved.
Add full jigger of Irish Whiskey for proper taste and body.
Top with a collar of whipped cream by pouring gently over a spoon.
Enjoy it while piping hot.

name: Sweet Spanish Coffee
directions: Rub lemon around the rim of the glass, then dip into a dish of raw sugar.
Warm brandy in a pan or a chafing dish. Pour into glass and ignite. Add jigger of Kahlua and ignite. Pour in hot coffee, leaving about 1 1/2". Add whipped cream, level to the top of the glass.

name: Traditional Irish Coffee
directions: Place whiskey and sugar in a warm coffee mug.  Stir the mixture until the sugar is dissolved.  Pour hot coffee into the mug, the layer a dollop of soft whipped cream on top.

You can adjust the amount of sugar to your liking.  To make soft whipped cream yourself, take heavy cream and whip it just until it thickens.  You do not want to whip the cream to the point where it forms peaks.  Slainte!

similar.name: Irish Coffee from the Buena Vista San Francisco
